name: Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail
name_original: Castillo de Selma y Sant Jaume de Montagut – circular desde Santes Creus
description: Experience the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail, a 23.1-mile (37.2 km) mountain biking route through vineyards and mountains.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aiguamúrcia offers diverse landscapes in the Catalonia Tarragona province. The region features varied terrain, including rocky sections and steep slopes, particularly within the Montmell mountain range. Riders can experience panoramic views encompassing vineyards and local villages. The area provides a blend of challenging routes and accessible paths, suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Aiguamúrcia The most popular mountain bike trail is Penedès 360 Slow Bike: Torrelles de Foix Loop, an easy 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle introduction to the region's cycling paths. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail, a moderate 23.1 miles (37.2 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including ascents that reward with expansive views. Local mountain bikers also love the Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from el Pont d'Armentera, a 67.9 miles (109.3 km) trail leading through the Montmell mountain range, often completed in about 10 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Aiguamúrcia is defined by the Montmell mountain range, vineyards, and rocky sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult, long-distance challenges. The routes in Aiguamúrcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aiguamúrcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Aiguamúrcia?
A: There are over 180 mountain bike trails around Aiguamúrcia, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 27 easy routes, 129 moderate routes, and 26 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Aiguamúrcia?
A: Mountain biking in Aiguamúrcia features diverse terrain, particularly within the Montmell mountain range. You can expect rocky sections and steep slopes, which can be challenging but rewarding. The region also offers panoramic views of vineyards, local villages, and even the sea on clear days.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Aiguamúrcia?
A: Yes, Aiguamúrcia offers several easy mountain bike trails. A great option for a gentle introduction to the region's cycling paths is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969725894/penedes-360-slow-bike-torrelles-de-foix-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 Slow Bike: Torrelles de Foix Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.4 miles (15.1 km) long.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31569766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from el Pont d'Armentera&lt;/a&gt;. This extensive trail is 67.9 miles (109.3 km) long and leads through the Montmell mountain range, often taking over 10 hours to complete.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Aiguamúrcia offer access to several notable landmarks and natural features. You can explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castellot&lt;/a&gt;, the remains of a medieval fortress, or the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4135049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Miquel&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include various viewpoints offering stunning vistas, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico del Montmell&lt;/a&gt;, and natural springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4667909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font-rubí Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Aiguamúrcia?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Aiguamúrcia are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969725894/penedes-360-slow-bike-torrelles-de-foix-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 Slow Bike: Torrelles de Foix Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another longer circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33821450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Aiguamúrcia?
A: The mountain bike trails in Aiguamúrcia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of challenging ascents with rewarding panoramic views, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Is Aiguamúrcia suitable for mountain biking with an electric bike (e-MTB)?
A: Yes, electric bikes are noted to make some of the ascents in Aiguamúrcia more accessible. Given the region's steep slopes and challenging sections, an e-MTB can help riders with some experience reach summits and enjoy the breathtaking views without dismounting as frequently.

Q: What is a popular moderate mountain bike trail in the area?
A: A top favorite among local mountain bikers is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946107428/penedes-360-all-mountain-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 23.1 miles (37.2 km) path traverses varied terrain, including ascents that reward with expansive views.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Montmell mountain range specifically?
A: Yes, the Montmell mountain range is a focal point for many routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31597953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Monestir de Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt; is a long and challenging route that leads through this significant mountain range, offering extensive exploration of its natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Can I find natural water sources or springs along the trails?
A: Yes, the region features several natural water sources. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4667909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font-rubí Spring&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Dous Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Rector Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which can be refreshing stops during your ride.
tips: The Monastery of Santa María de Santes Creus is a Cistercian abbey built in the 12th century. The grandeur of the monastery is due to the donations received from the nobility who wanted to be buried here.

Abandoned town of Selma, in a beautiful place with good views, access to the same depopulated by mountain bike is not easy, although it is worth visiting. more information in this link https://pueblosdelolvido.com/selma-lleida/
Gothic-style church located in the municipality of Querlo, in Alt Camp. The façade has two steeple bell towers and the access door is made up of three arches.
Mount 963 meters high in the municipality of Querol. From the top, where you can find a geodesic vertex, you can see privileged views of the Alt Camp.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this trail are during spring and autumn. It's advisable to avoid the comparatively cold winters and the extremely hot summers for a more enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a diverse mix of surfaces, including dirt roads, farm tracks, paths, and small roads. You'll also encounter more rugged, technical sections with rocks, roots, and even steps, particularly on steep mountain slopes. The majority of the technical trails are downhill when ridden in the suggested direction.

faqs.question: Is the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not suitable for beginners. It demands a decent to very good level of fitness and advanced riding skills, especially for its more demanding stages and technical sections. Familiarity with technical trails is highly recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: A hardtail or full-suspension mountain bike is recommended due to the varied and often technical terrain, which includes rocks, roots, and steps.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and cultural sights. You'll traverse vineyard-filled plains, mountain ranges with views stretching to the sea, and pass through charming towns, castles, and farmhouses. Notable highlights include the Royal Monastery of Santes Creus, Selma Castle, the Church of Sant Jaume de Montagut, and Montagut Peak (962 m).

faqs.question: Where does the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail start and end?
faqs.answer: The trail is a loop that typically starts and ends in Sant Sadurní d'Anoia. This town is well-connected by train and bus, making it accessible for riders.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through various types of terrain, including agricultural areas and natural parks. While specific regulations for dogs may vary by section, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to always clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no general permit or entrance fee required to ride the Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail itself, as it utilizes public paths and roads. However, individual attractions or private properties along the route may have their own entry fees or access rules.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies), E05, GR-175 BTT, Ruta del Císter, and Sender de Santes Creus, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a multi-day ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and technical sections, you should carry essential spare parts for your bike. Additionally, pack sufficient water, food, navigation tools, first-aid supplies, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. Although the route is never too far from populated areas, being self-sufficient is key for a multi-day adventure.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for food and accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is designed to pass through charming towns and villages, ensuring access to water, food, and shelter. You'll find opportunities to stop at local cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options, especially in the more populated zones.

waypoints.name: Church of Sant Jaume de Montagut
